tldr.title: Echoes of the AI Winter
tldr.author: kchanqvq
bullets: Unrealistic ROI expectations on massive LLM CapEx, fueled by high Nvidia H100 costs, raise concerns of a looming market correction.
Frontier models face diminishing returns as scaling laws hit training data walls, prompting a pivot to synthetic data and distillation.
Practitioners are shifting focus from massive proprietary APIs to cost-efficient, open-weights small language models like Llama-3 and Phi-3.

tldr.title: What are you doing this weekend?
tldr.author: caius
bullets: Engineers use weekend downtime to experiment with new languages like Rust or Go and contribute to open-source repositories.
Weekend projects often focus on self-hosting, updating home lab Kubernetes clusters, or migrating personal databases and APIs.
Prioritizing complete cognitive rest over weekend coding is vital for long-term engineering productivity and preventing professional burnout.

tldr.title: usbliter8: An A12/A13 SecureROM exploit
tldr.author: jeanthomas
bullets: The usbliter8 hardware exploit targets the unpatchable bootrom of Apple A12 and A13 Bionic system-on-chips.
It targets USB control transfers during DFU mode, enabling arbitrary code execution before the iOS kernel loads.
Because SecureROM is read-only, Apple cannot patch this vulnerability, enabling permanent hardware-level decryption and jailbreaks.

tldr.title: Amazon EC2 C7a instances are now available in the Asia Pacific (Singapore) Region
tldr.author: aws@amazon.com
bullets: AWS C7a instances, powered by 4th Gen AMD EPYC Genoa CPUs, are now available in the Singapore region.
DevOps teams can attach up to 128 EBS volumes per C7a instance, a significant increase from C6aΓÇÖs limit of 28.
Built on AWS Nitro, the instances feature DDR5 memory, AVX-512, VNNI, and bfloat16 for high-performance workloads.

tldr.title: Microsoft adds another year to Windows 10 extended update program
tldr.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullets: Microsoft extended the Windows 10 Extended Security Update (ESU) program as the OS retains roughly 25% of PC market share.
The extension offers security teams additional time to patch legacy Windows 10 endpoints before forcing a Windows 11 migration.
Organizations should plan budgets for paid ESU licensing or accelerate hardware procurement to meet strict Windows 11 TPM requirements.
